BOISE, July ARREST of certain
witnesses in the Haywood trial who
have testified for the defense on
perjury charges is certain to come.
Agents of the prosecution are now
searching the Coenr d'Alene dis
trict for evidence to contravert that
Of DR.. MCGEE, who swore positively
he met Orchard at Wallace in July
or August, 1904.


MCGEE seemed sure of his
ground, but the prosecution insists
he is mistaken. 1t is rumored that
northern Idaho authorities are pre-
pairing information seeking the ar
rest of w. F. (Big Bid Davis for
completely in blowing up the
Bunker Hill & Sullivan mill at
Wardner.


Max Malich, Denver saloon keep
err, was the first witness today. He
told about the strike at Globe
smelling work at Globeville, su.
burb of Denver. He swore he first
met Orchard in his store at the
time this strike began, and was In
troduced by the president Of the
union, who later turned out to be g
Pinkerton man.


Orchard played cards in the back
room of his saloon and tried tC
start poker game, which Malich
said he stopped. Witness denied
absolutely he ever tried to hire
Orchard to blow up the Globe no
tel, which was occupied by strike
breakers of the smelter trust.


He swore the trust started an op
position store to his because he re
fused to refrain from extending
credit to union men. He also testi
fed Orchard several times told him
Steunenberg caused him to lose his
fortune and he would get even II
he swung for it.


Following an attempt to throw
acid on him as he went to bed, Ma
Itch swore Orchard came to him
and offered to kill the manager oil
the opposition store for money
enough to get out of town. He re
fused to permit anything Of the
kind.


Malich's place was burned after
he left 1t one day to visit his
ranch.


Borah questioned him consider
ably along the line OF Orchards
connection with the fire, the appar
ent inference being that Orchard
burned it for the insurance. Ma
Itch denied the goods were removed
from his store and taken to Petit
bones prior to the fire.


He also denied that Orchard
came to him after the killing O
Lyle Gregory and told him II hi
